entries.name: Dagger Cloud
entries.description: Used as the remote Dagger engine for in-cluster manifest rendering Jobs. Eliminates the need for a privileged in-cluster Dagger engine deployment.

entries.name: distroless/static
entries.description: Minimal, non-root container images; eliminates shell attack surface

entries.name: kind
entries.description: Zero-cloud-cost local Kubernetes; standard for CI and dev cluster parity

entries.name: k3d
entries.description: Lighter alternative to kind; monitor for full API compatibility improvements

entries.name: minikube
entries.description: Heavier than kind for single-node dev; inconsistent Docker driver behaviour

entries.name: chi
entries.description: Selected as standard Go HTTP router for all services - stdlib-compatible, OTel-native, lightweight

entries.name: gin
entries.description: Popular alternative - rejected due to custom Context type breaking stdlib compatibility

entries.name: echo
entries.description: Popular alternative - similar tradeoffs to gin, no compelling advantage over chi

entries.name: huma
entries.description: OpenAPI-first approach - too opinionated for general use but worth monitoring

entries.name: log/slog (Go stdlib)
entries.description: Formal adoption of slog as the platform logging library

entries.name: go.opentelemetry.io/contrib/bridges/otelslog
entries.description: Official OTel bridge for slog to enable OTLP log export

entries.name: zerolog
entries.description: No official OTel bridge; not adopted

entries.name: zap
entries.description: Official OTel bridge exists, but slog preferred for stdlib compatibility

entries.name: servicedefaults pattern
entries.description: New initialization pattern for Go services; analogous to .NET Aspire AddServiceDefaults()

entries.name: shellcheck
entries.description: Static analysis for bash/shell scripts. Catches syntax errors, unsafe patterns, and portability issues before they reach production. Adopted as the standard linting tool for all .sh files in the platform, consistent with the platform's quality tooling posture for Go (golangci-lint) and TypeScript (ESLint).

entries.name: dagger
entries.description: Existing CI/CD platform extended with Render and PublishModule tasks

entries.name: gRPC
entries.description: High-performance RPC framework for internal service-to-service communication with native streaming and multiplexing support

entries.name: Protocol Buffers (protobuf)
entries.description: Efficient binary serialization format and schema language for gRPC services ensuring backward compatibility and cross-language support

entries.name: genqlient
entries.description: Type-safe GraphQL code generator for Go; chosen for compile-time safety and consistency across services

entries.name: bats-core
entries.description: Bash Automated Testing System — the de-facto standard test harness for bash scripts. Adopted to complete the quality tooling triad for bash (alongside shellcheck linting and shfmt formatting), consistent with the platform's posture that every language/runtime must have automated testing, linting, and formatting enforced in CI.

entries.name: Prometheus ServiceMonitor
entries.description: Kubernetes ServiceMonitor CRD for Prometheus auto-discovery and metric collection configuration

entries.name: shfmt
entries.description: Formatter for bash/shell scripts. Enforces consistent style and catches structural issues. Adopted as the standard formatting tool for all .sh files in the platform, consistent with the quality tooling posture for Go (gofmt) and TypeScript (Prettier).

entries.name: Grafana
entries.description: Unified dashboarding platform for logs, metrics, and traces correlation

entries.name: Grafana Provisioning API
entries.description: Dashboard and datasource management via API and configuration files

entries.name: Tempo
entries.description: Grafana Labs OSS; strong OTel support; not yet CNCF; Trial until production validation

entries.name: OpenTelemetry Go SDK
entries.description: Standard Go library for instrumenting applications with traces, metrics, and logs; enables vendor-neutral observability

entries.name: OpenTelemetry
entries.description: CNCF standard; vendor-agnostic instrumentation SDK for all Go services

entries.name: Prometheus
entries.description: Industry-standard metrics; native Kubernetes integration

entries.name: Loki
entries.description: De-facto log aggregation for Kubernetes/Grafana environments; LogQL familiar to Prometheus users

entries.name: kube-prometheus-stack
entries.description: Opinionated helm chart bundling Prometheus + AlertManager + Grafana; community standard

entries.name: KubeVela
entries.description: CNCF project; OAM standard alignment; production-proven but new to this platform; advancing to Adopt when observability integration validated

entries.name: Open Application Model (OAM)
entries.description: CNCF standard for cloud-native application definition; aligns with long-term vendor neutrality goals

entries.name: vela CLI
entries.description: KubeVela's CLI tool; Trial aligned with KubeVela platform entry

entries.name: kubectl
entries.description: Already added to Tech Radar in support-kubernetes; this change implements the adoption

entries.name: skaffold
entries.description: Already added to Tech Radar in support-kubernetes; this change implements the adoption

entries.name: k9s
entries.description: Already added to Tech Radar in support-kubernetes; this change implements the adoption

entries.name: helm
entries.description: Already added to Tech Radar in support-kubernetes; this change implements the adoption

entries.name: Trivy
entries.description: Best-in-class OSS container scanner; single binary, wide CVE coverage, CycloneDX SBOM

entries.name: Grype
entries.description: Strong alternative to Trivy; monitor for capability parity

entries.name: Snyk
entries.description: SaaS lock-in; free tier limits; not suitable for this platform's vendor-agnosticism goals

entries.name: Grafana Loki
entries.description: Efficient label-based log aggregation; lighter than ELK

entries.name: Grafana Tempo
entries.description: Grafana-native distributed tracing; supersedes Jaeger for this stack

entries.name: Lightstep
entries.description: SaaS vendor lock-in; cost at scale; rejected in this evaluation

entries.name: Jaeger
entries.description: Superseded by Tempo for Grafana-native workflows

entries.name: Kubernetes
entries.description: De-facto standard for container orchestration; CNCF Graduated; required for component platform and observability stack

entries.name: Prettier (Markdown)
entries.description: Industry-standard formatter with native markdown support; already in the npm ecosystem for this repo

entries.name: markdownlint-cli2
entries.description: Widely adopted markdown structure linter with a rich configurable rule set; npm-native and CI-friendly

entries.name: Vale
entries.description: Language-agnostic prose linter with pluggable style packages; runs as a standalone binary without runtime dependencies

entries.name: cspell
entries.description: Fast npm-native spell checker with project wordlist support; handles technical terminology well via custom dictionaries

entries.name: lychee
entries.description: Fast parallel link checker with configurable retries, exclusion patterns, and native markdown support; runs as a standalone binary

entries.name: playwright-extra
entries.description: Stealth plugin wrapper for Playwright — useful for anti-detection scraping but not yet Adopt due to limited maintenance signals

entries.name: playwright-extra-plugin-stealth
entries.description: Community stealth plugin that suppresses webdriver fingerprints; pair with playwright-extra

entries.name: Bubble Tea
entries.description: De-facto Go TUI framework using the Elm Architecture. 40k stars, 18k+ dependents, corporate-backed by Charm. v2.0.0 released Feb 2026. Selected as the standard TUI library for staccato-toolkit/tui.

entries.name: cobra
entries.description: The de-facto Go CLI framework. 43k stars, 202k dependent repos, used by Kubernetes, Helm, GitHub CLI, Docker. Selected as the CLI framework for staccato-toolkit/cli.

entries.name: go-app
entries.description: Go+WASM PWA framework where a single Go codebase compiles to both the HTTP server and the WASM client. First-class PWA support (manifest, service worker, offline). 8.9k stars, actively maintained (Feb 2026). Trial pending production validation of WASM binary size and load performance.

entries.name: prometheus-client
entries.description: Prometheus client libraries (Go, Python, JavaScript); standard for metrics instrumentation across all service languages

entries.name: GraphQL
entries.description: Query language and runtime for API consumption; standardized for consistent client integration patterns

entries.name: graphql-core
entries.description: Core GraphQL schema and validation library; adopted for Go backend GraphQL implementation

entries.name: @opentelemetry/sdk-node
entries.description: Node.js OpenTelemetry SDK — already adopted for Go services; extending to Backstage Node.js backend

entries.name: @opentelemetry/exporter-prometheus
entries.description: Prometheus metric exporter for OTel Node SDK; aligns with existing Prometheus stack

entries.name: @opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-http
entries.description: OTLP/HTTP trace exporter; routes Backstage traces to Jaeger via the OTLP protocol

entries.name: @opentelemetry/auto-instrumentations-node
entries.description: Auto-instrumentation for Node.js (Express, HTTP, etc.); zero-code span generation for Backstage backend

entries.name: Grafana Alloy
entries.description: Next-gen OpenTelemetry-native collector from Grafana Labs; replaces Promtail and standalone OTel Collector with a single programmable pipeline agent. Trial until production validation of River syntax and Kubernetes log collection stability.

entries.name: Headlamp
entries.description: Selected as the platform's web-based Kubernetes UI. CNCF sandbox project, Apache 2.0 licensed, plugin-extensible, and supports CRD display natively. No cluster-side agents required for core functionality.

entries.name: Penpot
entries.description: Open-source, self-hosted UI/UX design and prototyping platform. Deployed via Helm on kind-staccato-dev for collaborative design work alongside development workflows.

entries.name: Flux v2
entries.description: GitOps sync engine. Pull-based, CNCF Graduated. OCIRepository source replaces GitRepository/Gitea source from prior flux-local-bootstrap design.

entries.name: Harbor
entries.description: OCI registry receiving pushed rendered manifests via flux push artifact

entries.name: Flux Operator
entries.description: ControlPlane's Flux Operator manages Flux controller lifecycle via the FluxInstance CRD. Deployed as a custom KubeVela addon wrapping the OCI Helm chart. Provides a declarative interface for Flux configuration via the FluxInstance CRD.

entries.name: OAM / KubeVela
entries.description: Open Application Model. KubeVela addons used to manage platform infrastructure (Harbor, Flux Operator) alongside OAM Applications for workloads. Remains Trial — expanding usage to include the addon mechanism for platform component lifecycle.

entries.name: k0s
entries.description: Lightweight, single-binary Kubernetes distribution by Mirantis. Replaces KinD for local dev and targets production deployments via k0sctl. Provides dev-to-prod parity without a separate control-plane host requirement in single-node mode.

entries.name: k0sctl
entries.description: CLI tool for deploying and managing k0s clusters declaratively via a YAML config. Used as the provisioning layer in the bootstrap CLI command.

entries.name: Flux HelmRelease
entries.description: Flux v2 HelmRelease CRD used to manage third-party Helm chart installations declaratively via GitOps. Replaces ad-hoc helm install scripts for the observability stack and Gitea.

entries.name: Flux CD
entries.description: GitOps reconciliation engine for staccato-manifests; drives rollout lifecycle via Kustomization health checks.

entries.name: Progressive Delivery (canary + blue-green)
entries.description: Weight-based canary and atomic blue-green deployment patterns expressed as OAM traits; trialing before full platform adoption.

entries.name: Flux v2 Kustomization CRD
entries.description: Flux `Kustomization` objects drive the GitOps reconciliation loop from `staccato-manifests` into the cluster. Adopting as the standard wiring layer for all component deployments.

entries.name: KubeVela ComponentDefinition (CUE)
entries.description: CUE-based ComponentDefinition as the platform-standard abstraction layer for Kubernetes workload types; promotes intent-driven OAM Applications over raw YAML

entries.name: OAM Application manifest pattern
entries.description: Canonical pattern for declaring Kubernetes workloads as OAM Applications — replaces raw Deployment YAML in source repos

entries.name: vela export (render pipeline)
entries.description: KubeVela OAM Application rendering step driven from Dagger

entries.name: Gitea
entries.description: Self-hosted Git service deployed via Helm into the local kind cluster; enables a fully local GitOps loop without requiring GitHub access for day-to-day development

entries.name: Kustomize
entries.description: Canonical first-party manifest renderer for staccato-toolkit; overlay model separates environment concerns without templating language overhead

entries.name: Flux
entries.description: GitOps sync engine reading from staccato-manifests to reconcile cluster state. Chosen over ArgoCD for its pull-based Kubernetes-native operator model and lightweight footprint.

entries.name: ArgoCD
entries.description: Rejected as primary GitOps engine: UI-heavy, higher operator overhead, not aligned to CLI/platform use case; may be revisited for UI-centric workloads

entries.name: Garden
entries.description: Content-hash action graph with cross-session caching, Mutagen sync mode for hot-reload, and CI portability via environment-scoped providers. Selected over Tilt for multi-service platforms where per-session full rebuilds become the bottleneck.

entries.name: Tilt
entries.description: Valid for small-scale single-developer workflows; fast setup; no cross-session caching. Revisit if Garden configuration overhead proves excessive at current service count.

entries.name: watchexec
entries.description: Already in use in dev container images (Containerfile.dev) to trigger in-container go build + restart on source file changes. Solid, language-agnostic, zero configuration.

entries.name: Telepresence
entries.description: CNCF Incubating. Surgical per-service integration-debug tool — intercepts cluster traffic to a local process. Evaluate separately when native debugger access to live cluster traffic becomes a development bottleneck.

entries.name: Go
entries.description: Go runtime adopted for authoring Dagger pipeline modules. Introduced by initialize-dagger-devops.

entries.name: koanf
entries.description: Multi-source config loader for Go; merges YAML files, dotenv, and env vars in explicit priority order with no global state

entries.name: invopop/jsonschema
entries.description: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 generation from Go struct definitions via reflection; struct is the single source of truth
